sketches.mechanism: Borrowing rules: you cannot move a value after it has been borrowed, and borrowed references have limited lifetimes
sketches.resonance: You can hold someone or let them go, but you cannot do both — Rust's ownership system enforces the impossibility of keeping what you've already given away.

sketches.mechanism: The 'finally' block always executes, even after a return — it is the code that runs no matter what happened
sketches.resonance: No matter how the story ends — whether we succeed, fail, or try to leave early — some things happen regardless.

sketches.mechanism: An uninitialized variable holds whatever was left in memory by a previous process — its value is undefined but never truly empty
sketches.resonance: A new name in an old room still holds the presence of whoever was there before; nothing is ever truly blank.
